conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

Let me start by reading the official guidance to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now let me help you work out your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To start, could you please tell me: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I have one child. His name is Alex and he's 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. So you have one child, Alex, who is 8 years old.

Now I need to ask: **Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I live in Bristol.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great. Now I need to understand your living arrangements with Alex.

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question to complete my assessment:

**Are you or your partner (if you have one) a Crown servant, or do you have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or are there any special circumstances regarding your residency status in the UK?**

(If none of these apply to you, just let me know and that's absolutely fine - most people won't have any of these circumstances.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of those apply to me. I'm just a regular UK resident, nothing special about my situation really.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for answering all my questions. Based on the information you've provided, I now have enough to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

Let me review your situation against the official rules:

- You have one child, Alex, who is 8 years old (under 16)
- You live in the UK (Bristol)
- Alex lives with you at home
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- You are a regular UK resident

According to the UK government guidance, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. You're usually considered responsible for a child if you live with them, which you do.

Since Alex is under 16, lives with you, and nobody else is claiming for him, you meet all the eligibility criteria.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

You meet all the requirements:
- Alex is under 16 (he's 8 years old)
- You live in the UK
- Alex lives with you
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him

You can go ahead and apply for Child Benefit. Please note that only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child, so as long as nobody else claims for Alex, you should be able to receive it.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK (Bristol), and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. The claimant is responsible for Alex as they live together. According to gov.uk guidance, a person normally qualifies for Child Benefit if they are responsible for a child under 16 and live in the UK. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 8-year-old son Alex. The claimant lives in the UK, Alex lives with them, Alex is under 16, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All standard eligibility requirements are satisfied.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 8 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK (Bristol), and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. The claimant is responsible for Alex as they live together. According to gov.uk guidance, a person normally qualifies for Child Benefit if they are responsible for a child under 16 and live in the UK. All eligibility criteria are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 8-year-old son Alex. The claimant lives in the UK, Alex lives with them, Alex is under 16, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All standard eligibility requirements are satisfied.
